zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Le Barker於1322年出生在英國史洛普爾其其時，他的父親Sir William De Caverall28歲，母親Mary Coleclough27歲。他於1346年與Alice Seisdon在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1368年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾阿德利享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Le Barker于1322年出生在英国史洛普爾其其时，他的父亲Sir William De Caverall28岁，母亲Mary Coleclough27岁。他于1346年与Alice Seisdon在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1368年在英国英格兰史洛普爾阿德利享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Barker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a tanner of leather, from Middle English barkere ‘tanner’, tree bark having been used as the tanning agent.

English: occupational name for a shepherd, from Middle English berker, bercher (Old French berchier, bercher, berkier, berker, Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’, genitive berbicis). With the change of -ar- to -er- in Middle English, this became indistinguishable from the preceding name (see 1 above).
Americanized form of German   Berger   or   Barger  .

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Le Barker was born in 1322, in Whitchurch, Shropshire, England, his father, Sir William De Caverall, was 28 and his mother, Mary Coleclough, was 27. He married Alice Seisdon in 1346, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1368, in Adderley, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Le Barker est né en 1322, à Whitchurch, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William De Caverall, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mary Coleclough, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Alice Seisdon en 1346, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1368, à Adderley, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Le Barker 1322 in Whitchurch, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William De Caverall 28 und seine Mutter Mary Coleclough 27. Er hat 1346 in Shropshire, England Alice Seisdon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1368 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Adderley, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Le Barker è nato il nel 1322 nel Whitchurch, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William De Caverall, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Mary Coleclough, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Alice Seisdon nel 1346, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1368, nel Adderley, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Le Barkerが1322年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウィトチャーチで生まれた時，父Sir William De Caverallは28歳，母Mary Colecloughは27歳であった。1346年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでAlice Seisdonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1368年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアアッドエルリーで46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Le Barker(이)가 1322년 영국 슈럽실르 휘트c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William De Caverall(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Coleclough(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1346년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Alice Seisdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1368년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 애델리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Le Barker nasceu em 1322, em Whitchurch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William De Caverall, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Mary Coleclough, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Seisdon em 1346, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1368, em Adderley, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Le Barker родился в 1322 г. в местности уичурч, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William De Caverall было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Coleclough, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Seisdon в 1346 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1368 г. в местности аддирли, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger Le Barker nació en 1322, en Whitchurch, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William De Caverall, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Mary Coleclough, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Alice Seisdon en 1346, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1368, en Adderley, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años.
